sections.heading: The BST rule
sections.body: A **Binary Search Tree** is a tree with one simple rule at every node: **everything to the left is smaller, everything to the right is bigger**. That rule unlocks O(log n) lookup, insertion, and deletion — when the tree stays balanced.

sections.title: Like a dictionary
sections.body: Look up a word. Open the middle. Word is earlier? Look left half. Later? Look right half. Each step halves the search space. That's what a BST does at every level.

sections.caption: Notice: left children are smaller, right children are larger.
Classic BST search — recursion meets the sorting rule.

questions.question: In a BST, where do values smaller than a node go?
questions.explanation: Left for smaller, right for larger. That invariant is the entire point.
